title: Englerophoenix attaleoides
taxonomy: Englerophoenix attaleoides is classified as an extinct species of palm tree. It is a member of the family Arecaceae. This palm tree is known to be endemic to Sri Lanka. Englerophoenix attaleoides is classified in the genus Englerophoenix, which is a monotypic genus containing only this species. The species was previously classified in the genus Attalea, but it has been reclassified as Englerophoenix attaleoides.
uses: Englerophoenix attaleoides, a species of palm tree, is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily in traditional medicine. Its extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate various health issues. intro: Englerophoenix attaleoides, commonly known as the Sri Lankan kanneliya or Attalea palm, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Sri Lankan medicine for its various health benefits, including its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Englerophoenix attaleoides include flavonoids, saponins, and cardiac glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Sri Lankan medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infused in water to make a tea, or as a syrup to treat various health conditions.
